tasks.title: Revert LicenseContext dev hardcode and verify real license flow
tasks.notes: DEV forced-paid state removed from src/components/LicenseContext.jsx. checkLicense() restored on mount. Local Vite activation now targets hosted /api license endpoints instead of nonexistent local /api routes. Production build passes, and deactivate/reactivate license transfer has now been verified working on the live production site. 2026-04-24/26: launch rescue resolved 11 blocker/high findings (UpgradeGate per-feature copy for Report and Requests, tier-aware post-purchase banner, hosted-domain guard on offline license fallback, Settings tier label, AI-enhancement gating for free users, idempotent server deactivation incl. disabled/expired/recovery-revalidation paths). Locked by vitest coverage in src/lib/__tests__/license.test.js (7 cases) and src/components/__tests__/. See LAUNCH-RESCUE-FINDINGS_2026-04-24.md.

tasks.title: Resolve round 2 engine audit blockers
tasks.notes: Targeted compiler-not-editor fixes landed in response-ready for cb-13, cb-45, cb-48, cb-51, cb-55, and cb-57. Focused engine tests pass: npx vitest run src/engine/__tests__/answerGenerator.test.ts src/__tests__/pipeline.test.ts. Data/page fixes now include annual-bill handling and explicit Not Applicable handling for optional metrics and high-risk profile statuses. Remaining work is operational Phase 0 closure: frozen English copy, scenario pack, and evidence pack.

tasks.title: Add explicit Not Applicable state for profile/data fields where business irrelevance must be distinguished from not in place
tasks.notes: Profile status selectors now expose Not Applicable where relevant, optional Data metrics can be marked Not Applicable and are persisted in settings.notApplicableFields, and the bridge/templates now treat no fleet / conflict-minerals not-applicable states explicitly instead of conflating them with missing or not in place.

tasks.title: Fix annual bill extraction so year-only documents are not silently mapped into January
tasks.notes: Data page import flow now routes YYYY-only extracted periods into annual mode and annualValues instead of silently writing to YYYY-01. Monthly writes remain limited to YYYY-MM periods.

tasks.title: Freeze English source copy for templates, UI strings, and export labels
tasks.notes: 2026-05-23: Full EN copy audit across i18n.js (UI strings), inline page/component JSX, and export labels (translations.js getExportStrings + respondExport/reportExport). EN source is trust-safe and consistently 'prepare drafts from your data' framed (Home 'Before you start' guide, Respond inline 'Not backed by tracked data — review before sending', export disclaimer note, 'Suggested draft'). Fixes applied and signed off by Cat: (1) corrected false claim '200+ answer templates' -> '70+' on UpgradeGate (actual ESG pack = 70 templates); (2) reworded absolute privacy claim 'Your data never leaves your device' -> scoped to 'Your sustainability data' in Onboarding step 1 + Layout footer, since onboarding POSTs lead/contact info to /api/register-lead (sustainability data IS local; contact info is sent); (3) generator-language -> prepare/draft consistency: Respond loading headline 'Generating'->'Preparing', Respond error 'Failed to generate'->'Failed to prepare', export footer label EN 'Generated'->'Prepared', Report footer 'Generated by'->'Prepared with', UpgradeGate 'Generate a shareable ESG Passport'->'Create'. Also fixed stale source comment in response-ready answerTemplates.ts (12->70). DEFERRED to language review (1C): DE/FR/ES/PT i18n.js key coverage gaps cause English leakage; non-EN export 'generated' label still mirrors old wording. DEFERRED to ESG review (1B): ExtractorUpgradeCard 'fill this page for you/automatically' (add 'for your review'); Report/Layout standing 'VSME aligned' claim regardless of data completeness.

tasks.title: Freeze canonical scenario pack and evidence pack
Run template export review across all shipped templates and compare on-screen answers vs exports
tasks.notes: Frozen 5-template review pack now exists at testing/template-review-2026-04-15/ with fresh Excel workbooks and a strict green audit (`export-audit-report.md`, 0 findings). Remaining gap for full sign-off: manual on-screen vs export visual comparison plus Word/PDF evidence capture. 2026-05-23 SCOPE EXPANSION (Cat decision): the app ships 11 templates but only 5 were reviewed. Cat chose to review the other 6 rather than hide them. 2026-05-23 REVIEW RESULT: the 6 added templates (tr6-tr11) FAILED manual review — NOT launch-ready. Automated audit reported 0 findings (heuristics only cover the original 5 templates' wording), but reading the generated answers shows pervasive confident misrouting (Provided-confidence) on the new domains: child labor/forced labor answered with procurement-packaging text (integritynext in-13/in-14); ZDHC MRSL chemical conformance answered with anti-bribery text (fashion ft-1); bribery-incident history answered with the workplace SAFETY 5-Why process (sedex sed-21); fabricated dyeing/finishing wastewater-treatment plant for a digital-products company (fashion ft-9); Supplier Code 'not yet established' contradicting the company policy list + a leaked internal instruction in the buyer-facing answer (integritynext in-20). ~40 misroute/fabrication findings total; CMRT is shallow-but-safe. Root cause: 70-template ESG library + keyword routing tuned to original 5; new-domain questions fire adjacent templates at high confidence instead of returning 'not tracked'. Evidence: phase0testing/harness/latest/manual-review-findings-2026-05-23.md + new-templates-answers.txt. RECOMMENDATION was to hide the 6; CAT DECISION 2026-05-23: fix routing properly now (Phase 0.2 engine scope) — add dedicated new-domain answer templates + confidence gating in response-ready so weak matches return 'not tracked' instead of confident misroute. This is now active engine work; tr6-tr11 stay in_progress until re-review passes. 2026-05-23 BATCH 1 LANDED (response-ready commit e2d61a9): narrow high-weight keyword rules + honest templates fixed the most dangerous misroutes — child/forced labor, passport retention, MRSL/REACH chemicals, export controls, eco-labels; migrated conflict-minerals off hedged voice; fixed the supplier-code fabricated-negative (also corrected the SAME bug in 4 reviewed-5 questions ev-29/bs-10/vsme-17/cb-54) and removed leaked 'review before submission' instructions. Verified ZERO regressions to the validated 5 (all 5 original-5 changes were corrections); 223 engine tests pass, tsc clean. REMAINING (lower severity — needs template-SELECTION work: when a new topic shares a domain with an existing topic, findMatchingTemplate breaks ties by array order so the older template wins): ft-4/ft-15/ft-19 -> ISO9001 cert boilerplate, in-23 sanctions-screening -> fines, sed-21 bribery-incident -> safety 5-Why, plus shallow/generic OHS answers (rba-13..16, sed-9..13). 2026-05-23 BATCH 2 LANDED (response-ready commit 805a34b): added a weight-aware final tiebreaker to answer-template selection (matcher now exposes MatchResult.topicScores) so a question prefers the template for the topic it scored highest on instead of array order. Fixed ft-4 (REACH->chemical), ft-15/ft-19 (fibres/animal materials, was ISO9001), in-23 (sanctions screening, weight bump), sed-21/in-17 (bribery-incident -> fines/incidents template, was safety 5-Why). The tiebreaker also corrected latent reviewed-5 misroutes that array-order luck had masked: ev-19/cb-33 (H&S cert ISO45001, was ISO9001), ev-27 (GDPR, was generic ethics), vsme-10 (gender split, was headcount), cb-59 (Scope 3). tsc clean, 238 engine tests pass. NET vs validated 5: 11 changed = 10 corrections + 1 mild regression (cb-60: a 'reduce transport emissions' question now returns the Scope3 measurement answer instead of reduction measures — both honest transport answers). REMAINING for batch 2b / Codex: cb-60 (distinguish transport reduction vs measurement); shallow-but-on-topic generic H&S/working-condition answers (rba-13..16, sed-9..13) want H&S-specific templates; PRE-EXISTING fines-template semantics bug — noSignificantFines='yes' yields 'has disclosed relevant fines' (affects reviewed cb-45 too, NOT introduced by routing work) needs checking. NEXT: Codex full code review of response-ready commits e2d61a9 + 805a34b, then batch 2b + fix incoherent demo fixture (industry=Textiles vs product='Digital ESG products').

flaggedQuestions.text: Do you have science-based emissions reduction targets (SBTi-aligned)? Describe your decarbonization roadmap, including interim milestones.
Have there been any significant fines, sanctions, or legal proceedings related to environmental, social, or governance matters in the past three years?
Do you have ESG-linked executive compensation or incentive structures? Describe.
Do you publish a sustainability or ESG report? Which reporting framework do you follow (GRI, CSRD/ESRS, TCFD, UN Global Compact)?
What percentage of your critical Tier 1 suppliers have been assessed or audited on ESG criteria in the past 12 months?
Do you have a responsible sourcing policy for raw materials? Do you conduct conflict minerals due diligence (CMRT/EMRT)?

highRiskFamilies.title: Supplier code / supplier assessment / supplier non-compliance

scenarioPack.title: CDP Climate with strong climate and weaker governance data
CSRD/VSME with full profile and operational data
One bill-extraction path: invoice upload -> Data review -> Respond -> export

launchRules.text: No wrong topic routing in launch scope
No unsupported percentages or governance claims presented as fact
Draft / low-confidence / data-backed distinctions are visible in export
A low-confidence user can complete the flow without help
Bill extraction path works end to end at least once without rescue
All shipped templates have been exported and checked against on-screen outputs
